name: PM Awas Yojana (Urban)
description: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Urban provides financial assistance for construction or enhancement of pucca houses for EWS, LIG, and MIG families in urban areas.
eligibility: EWS (annual income up to ₹3 lakh), LIG (₹3–6 lakh), MIG-I (₹6–12 lakh), MIG-II (₹12–18 lakh). Beneficiary family must not own a pucca house anywhere in India.
benefits: Interest subsidy of 3–6.5% on home loans. Credit Linked Subsidy up to ₹2.67 lakh for EWS/LIG.

name: Ayushman Bharat – PM Jan Arogya Yojana
description: World's largest government-funded health insurance scheme providing coverage of ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalisation.
eligibility: BPL families identified from SECC 2011 data. Covers over 10.74 crore families. No premium to be paid by beneficiaries.
benefits: Health coverage up to ₹5 lakh per family per year. Covers 1,929 medical procedures including surgeries and daycare treatments. Cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals.

name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
description: Direct income support of ₹6,000 per year provided to small and marginal farmer families in three equal instalments of ₹2,000 each.
eligibility: All small and marginal farmer families across the country. Beneficiary must own cultivable land. Excludes income tax payers, government employees, and high-income earners.
benefits: ₹6,000 per year (₹2,000 every 4 months) directly to bank account via DBT.

name: PM Jan Dhan Yojana
description: National mission for financial inclusion providing basic banking and financial services to all Indian citizens, especially the unbanked.
eligibility: All Indian citizens above 10 years of age. No minimum balance required. Available across all bank branches and Business Correspondents.
benefits: Zero-balance savings account, RuPay debit card, ₹2 lakh accident insurance cover, ₹30,000 life insurance cover, overdraft facility up to ₹10,000.

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
description: A small savings scheme for the girl child under the Beti Bachao Beti Padhao campaign, offering attractive interest rates and tax benefits.
eligibility: Girl child below 10 years of age. Account opened by natural/legal guardian. Maximum 2 accounts per family (one per girl child). One account allowed for twins/triplets.
benefits: Current interest rate 8.2% p.a. (one of the highest). Tax exemption under Section 80C up to ₹1.5 lakh. Maturity amount used for education or marriage.

name: PM Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
description: Provides micro and small enterprise loans to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises through various financial institutions.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen with a business plan for non-farm income-generating activity. Three categories: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishore (₹50,000–5 lakh), Tarun (₹5–10 lakh).
benefits: Collateral-free loans up to ₹10 lakh. No processing fee for Shishu loans. Mudra Card for working capital needs.

name: PM Ujjwala Yojana
description: Provides free LPG connections to women from BPL households to protect their health and shift from traditional biomass-based cooking.
eligibility: Women from BPL households. Beneficiary must not already have an LPG connection in the household. Identified through SECC-2011 data.
benefits: Free LPG connection with first refill. Security deposit waiver. EMI facility for stove and first refill purchase.

name: MGNREGS (Mahatma Gandhi NREGA)
description: Guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to rural households whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.
eligibility: Adult members of any rural household. Must apply for a Job Card at the local Gram Panchayat.
benefits: 100 days of guaranteed wage employment per year. Statutory minimum wages. Work must be provided within 15 days of application or unemployment allowance paid.

name: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao
description: A national campaign to address the declining Child Sex Ratio (CSR) and promote the welfare and education of the girl child.
eligibility: All families with girl children. Special focus on districts with low CSR across India.
benefits: Educational support for girl children. Awareness campaigns. Special scholarships and incentives for girl child education. Links to Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana.

name: PM Scholarship Scheme (PMSS)
description: Encourages technical and post-graduate education for the wards and widows of ex-servicemen, ex-Coast Guard personnel, and gallantry award winners.
eligibility: Wards/widows of ex-servicemen, ex-Coast Guard, and those killed/disabled in action. Must have secured minimum 60% marks in Class 12. Pursuing professional degree courses.
benefits: ₹3,000 per month for boys, ₹3,500 per month for girls. Scholarship for duration of professional course.
